summary: Excellent audience recording captures a high‑energy 1987 spring show highlighted by the rare Hand Jive and fiery Truckin', despite a few vocal slips on Morning Dew.
review: The show is praised as one of the best of the Spring ’87 East Coast run, featuring a tight post‑drums three‑song sequence, a standout Hand Jive (the only known performance) and a powerful Truckin' full of Phil’s bass bombs. The audience source is noted to be clearer than many contemporary soundboards, delivering an excellent listening experience, though Jerry’s mic failure on the final line of Morning Dew and a couple of lyrical slips are mentioned.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 5 stars, highlighting superb musical moments and an unusually clear audience recording; minor performance glitches keep it just shy of perfection.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience capture is described as clearer than the soundboard, with crisp balance throughout; only noted issue is a mic failure on the final lyric of Morning Dew.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert maintains steady, high‑energy momentum across both sets, highlighted by a taut post‑drums three‑song sequence and avoids the over‑blown jams that can plague other shows of the era.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mic failure on the final line of Morning Dew; otherwise his playing is not specifically commented on.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for a great bass roll throughout and “Phil bombs” during Truckin'.
band_member_comments.Bob: Forgot part of the lyrics to One More Saturday Night, noted as a slip.
